assertions.text: Output contains a markdown table (using | column | syntax)
assertions.evidence: | Scenario | Age | Credit Score | Income Status | Approved? | — full table present with pipe syntax

assertions.text: At least one output column name ends with '?' (e.g. 'Eligible?' or 'Approved?')
assertions.evidence: Column header 'Approved?' is present in the table

assertions.text: Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'creditScore', 'isEligible', 'boolean', 'int'
assertions.evidence: Columns are: Scenario, Age, Credit Score, Income Status, Approved? — all plain business language, no camelCase or type names

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row specifically for the senior applicant (65+) threshold scenario
assertions.evidence: Row 'Senior applicant, at senior threshold | 65 | 600 | Stable | Yes' directly tests the 600 threshold for seniors

assertions.text: The missing income scenario is represented — either as a row with an open/uncertain expected value (?, TBD, or blank) or as an explicit open question note
assertions.evidence: Row 'Standard applicant, missing income info | 40 | 720 | Unknown | ?' uses '?' in the Approved? column, and Open Question 1 explicitly discusses the gap

assertions.text: Scenario names describe conditions ('Senior applicant at threshold') not outcomes ('Approved')
assertions.evidence: Scenario names such as 'Senior applicant, at senior threshold', 'Standard applicant, good score, no income', 'Standard applicant, missing income info' all describe input conditions
